info.description:
Bank-to-bank coordination service implementing the inter-bank
transaction and OTC negotiation protocol. Authentication is
per-peer via the X-Api-Key header (see §2.10).
info.title: Interbank Service API

get.description:
Returns every cross-bank negotiation in which the
authenticated user is a party.

post.description:
The authenticated user becomes the buyer. The request is
forwarded to the seller's bank via §3.2; on success the
authoritative id assigned by the peer is returned.

delete.description: Closes the negotiation locally and notifies the peer via §3.5.

put.description:
Posts a counter-offer whether the authenticated user is the
buyer or the seller, notifying the opposing party's bank via §3.3.
put.summary: Post a counter-offer on a cross-bank negotiation

get.description:
Aggregates §3.1 /public-stock results from every peer in
the registry. Peers that fail are silently skipped.
get.summary: Browse public OTC stocks across all peer banks

post.description:
Single ingress endpoint for the bank-to-bank protocol (§2.11).
Accepts NEW_TX, COMMIT_TX and ROLLBACK_TX messages. The
X-Api-Key header authenticates the sending bank.

parameters.description: Peer bank API key issued to the caller
parameters.name: X-Api-Key

post.description:
§3.2 — peer bank's buyer initiates a negotiation against
a seller in our bank. Returns the ForeignBankId of the
new negotiation owned by us.

get.description:
§3.4 — returns the current negotiation state as the spec shape
(the OtcOffer fields plus isOngoing). The :rn path parameter is
the negotiation's authoritative (seller's) routing number.

put.description:
§3.3 — a peer bank posts a counter-offer (either party may
counter; the recipient may be the authoritative seller's bank
or the buyer's mirror-holding bank). Buyer/seller and ticker are
immutable; only negotiable parameters may change. The same party
may not counter twice in a row (turn rule).

delete.description:
§3.5 — either party may withdraw from a negotiation (the
recipient may be the authoritative or the mirror side). The
operation is idempotent: closing an already-closed negotiation
returns 204 without changing state.

get.description:
§3.6 — accepts the current offer on a negotiation owned by us
(we are the seller's authoritative bank). Forms the option
contract and drives the §2 NEW_TX that moves the premium, then
returns the resulting contract.

§3.1 — returns every stock holding at this bank that has
a non-zero public amount, grouped by ticker with the list
of sellers and their available quantities. Data pulled
from trading-service via gRPC.
get.summary: List public stocks at this bank

get.description:
§3.7 — peer banks call us to resolve one of our users into
a display name. routingNumber must match ours; id is the
local user id encoded as a decimal string.

buyerAccountNumber.description: BuyerAccountNumber is a Banka-4 extension to the protocol's OtcOffer.
BuyerAccountNumber is a Banka-4 extension to the protocol's OtcOffer.
